semanticCore: Valley, blood-money, flowing water, or paying
quranicRelevance: Valleys/gorges (Wadi - 'wadin ghayr thee zar''), and blood-money for accidental homicide (Diyah - 'diyatun musallamatun').

keyDerivations.meaning: Valley, riverbed, gorge, or hollow land
Blood-money, compensation, or fine for injury/homicide

figurativeOrRareUsages: فِي كُلِّ وَادٍ يَهِيمُونَ (26:225): Wandering in every valley (poets pursuing every flighty idea/style).
وَدِيَةٌ مُسَلَّمَةٌ إِلَى أَهْلِهِ (4:92): And blood-money handed over to his family (homicide compensation).
discardedNoise: Obscure details of valley plant species.
summaryEn: The root covers valleys and compensation, representing the physical valleys of the earth, the metaphorical wandering of poets, and legal blood-money (Diyah).
summaryAr: يغطي هذا الجذر الأودية والفداء المالي، ممثلاً أودية الأرض الجغرافية، وتيه الشعراء المجازي (في كل واد)، والدية المالية لقتل الخطأ (دية).
rawText: أَوْدَى بِهِ 4 It destroyed him; (T;) it removed him, or took him away: (M, K:) said of death [&c.]. (T, M, K.) See an ex. in art. بلو, conj. 8, and another voce أَزْلَمُ. === أَوْدَى: see a verse cited voce قَلَبَةٌ. === أَوْدَى العَيْرُ إِلّا ضَرِطًا: see ضَرَطَ. تَوْدِيَةٌ ; pl. تَوَادٍ, in the accus. case, by poetic licence, تَوَادِيًا: see a verse cited voce خُفٌّ. دِيَةٌ Bloodwit; a fine for bloodshed, i. e., homicide; consisting of a hundred camels. === دِيَاتُ جِرَاحَاتٍ [Fines for wounds]. (S, M, TA, &c., in art. شنق.) وَادٍ [A valley; a water-course, or torrent-bed: and sometimes a river;] a space intervening, (M, Msb, K,) i. e. any such space, (M, Msb,) between mountains or hills; (M, Msb, K;) through which a torrent runs forth [occasionally or constantly]: (Msb:) for which reason it is thus called. (M, Mgh, TA.) === حَلَّ بِوَادِيهِ i. q. بَاعَِ عَلَى بَيْعِهِ in one of the senses explained in art. بيع; i. e., ‡ He superseded him, &c. وَادَاهُ 3 inf. n. مُوَادَاةٌ, He took [from him] the دِيَة. (TA.) See 3 in art. عدو. وَدَى القَتِيلَ 1 He gave the bloodwit to the heir, or next of kin, of the slain person. (Msb.) وَدِىٌّ Small فَسِيل, q. v.; (S, Msb, K;) shoots, or offsets, cut off from palm-trees and planted: (Mgh:) young palm-trees. (TA.)
